title: Joints Object
description: The collection of joints in this component. This provides access to all existing joints and supports the ability to create new joints.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInferredJointInput
methods.description: Creates a joint input to define an inferred joint. Use functionality on the returned InferredJointInput to define the input needed to infer a joint.

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates a JointInput object, which is the API equivalent to the Joint command dialog. You you use methods and properties on the returned class to set the desired options, similar to providing input and setting options in the Joint command dialog. Once the settings are defined you call the Joints.add method passing in the JointInput object to create the actual joint.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified joint using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified joint using a name.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns number of joints in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

add.title: Joints.add Method
add.return_description: Returns the newly created Joint or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: The JointInput object that defines the geometry and various inputs that fully define a joint. A JointInput object is created using the Joints.createInput method.

related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a joint with ball joint motion

addInferredJoint.title: Joints.addInferredJoint Method
addInferredJoint.return_description: Returns the newly created Joint or fails if there is bad input.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: The InferredJointInput object that defines the pairs of geometry and other settings that Fusion will use to infer a joint from. An InferredJointInput object is created using the Joints.createInferredJointInput method.

classType.title: Joints.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInferredJointInput.title: Joints.createInferredJointInput Method
createInferredJointInput.description: Creates a joint input to define an inferred joint. Use functionality on the returned InferredJointInput to define the input needed to infer a joint.

createInput.title: Joints.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates a JointInput object, which is the API equivalent to the Joint command dialog. You you use methods and properties on the returned class to set the desired options, similar to providing input and setting options in the Joint command dialog. Once the settings are defined you call the Joints.add method passing in the JointInput object to create the actual joint.
createInput.return_description: Returns the JointInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: geometryOrOriginOne
parameters.description: A JointGeometry or JointOrigin object that defines the first set of geometry of the joint. JointGeometry objects are created by using the various static methods on the JointGeometry class and JointOrigin objects are created through the JointOrigins object.

parameters.name: geometryOrOriginTwo
parameters.description: A JointGeometry or JointOrigin object that defines the second set of geometry of the joint. JointGeometry objects are created by using the various static methods on the JointGeometry class and JointOrigin objects are created through the JointOrigins object.

item.title: Joints.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified joint using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: Joints.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified joint using a name.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid name was specified.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the item within the collection to return.

count.title: Joints.count Property
count.description: Returns number of joints in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = joints_var.count

isValid.title: Joints.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = joints_var.isValid

objectType.title: Joints.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():
